task_title: Load the Memory Layer demo showcase
user_prompt: Seed a relatable demo project so a new user can query, browse, and resume against real memories.
agent_summary: Seeded a self-referential showcase corpus of durable facts about Memory Layer itself.
notes: Self-referential demo corpus; safe to delete and reseed with `memory demo`.

structured_candidates.canonical_text: Memory Layer is a local-first memory system for coding agents: it captures durable project knowledge, curates it into canonical memories, and serves it back over a hybrid lexical + vector + graph retrieval path. The service, TUI, and web UI all read the same backend APIs.
structured_candidates.summary: Memory Layer captures, curates, and serves durable project memory locally.

sources.excerpt: hybrid lexical + vector + graph retrieval; shared backend APIs

structured_candidates.canonical_text: The backend service auto-applies its embedded SQL migrations on startup and requires PostgreSQL with the pgvector extension. It binds 127.0.0.1:4040 by default and serves the web UI from the first web/dist-style directory it discovers.
structured_candidates.summary: The service needs Postgres + pgvector, migrates on boot, and binds 4040.

structured_candidates.canonical_text: Retrieval strengthens the memories that earn it: every access raises a memory's activation, activation spreads to linked memories, and it decays over time. This ACT-R-grounded reinforcement is deterministic and needs no LLM.
structured_candidates.summary: Reinforcement raises activation on use, spreads to links, and decays over time.

sources.excerpt: access-driven activation with spreading and decay; deterministic

structured_candidates.canonical_text: Consolidation clusters related memories by relation edges, embedding similarity, and co-access, then proposes a single insight memory summarizing the cluster. Insights are human-gated: nothing is written without approval, and member memories keep living.
structured_candidates.summary: Consolidation proposes human-gated insight memories from clusters of related memories.

structured_candidates.canonical_text: Loop automations run through the local service control plane and produce reports, approval requests, or reviewable proposals rather than bypassing the Memory APIs. Built-ins cover context-pack refresh, memory hygiene, CI triage, and evaluation.
structured_candidates.summary: Loop automations are approval-gated and run through the service control plane.

sources.excerpt: reports, approval requests, or reviewable proposals; never bypass Memory APIs

structured_candidates.canonical_text: The built-in MCP server is read-first: it exposes query, resume, overview, activities, and memory-inspection tools for agents, while write and loop-control tools require approval. HTTP MCP is protected by the service API token.
structured_candidates.summary: The MCP server is read-first; memory tools read, loop tools require approval.

structured_candidates.canonical_text: Decision: lexical keyword search works with no API keys, so the deterministic retrieval paths run fully local and offline; LLM and embedding providers are optional and only improve curation and semantic search.
structured_candidates.summary: Keyless lexical retrieval is the default; LLM/embeddings are optional upgrades.

structured_candidates.canonical_text: Convention: memories should be short, inspectable, and useful without reading the original chat — a durable claim plus its scope and evidence. Weak memories record activity ('looked at runtime.rs'); strong ones record the durable outcome.
structured_candidates.summary: Good memories state a durable claim with scope and evidence, not raw activity.

sources.excerpt: durable claim + scope + evidence; outcome over activity

structured_candidates.canonical_text: The Graph tab keeps the isolation radius client-side and anchors traversal on the selected node, so expanding or isolating a neighborhood never re-queries the backend. The 3D force graph layers code, provenance, and relationship edges.
structured_candidates.summary: The web Graph tab keeps isolate-depth client-side, anchored on the selection.

sources.excerpt: isolate_depth stays local; traversal anchored on selected node

structured_candidates.canonical_text: Code-graph extraction stores files, symbols, and references as graph nodes and edges, linking imports, callers, and memory provenance. Graph-aware retrieval uses these edges to surface memories connected to the code a query touches.
structured_candidates.summary: Code-graph extraction stores files/symbols/references and powers graph-aware retrieval.

sources.excerpt: files, symbols, references; imports, callers, memory provenance

structured_candidates.canonical_text: Repo-local skills resolve the source checkout's dev CLI before an installed memory binary, so a running dev service is used in preference to the packaged one. This avoids 401 invalid-api-token errors from mixing dev and prod services.
structured_candidates.summary: Repo-local skills prefer the dev CLI to avoid mixed dev/prod token errors.

sources.excerpt: prefer source checkout dev CLI; avoids 401 from mixed services

structured_candidates.canonical_text: The public documentation site is a separate Next.js + Fumadocs app rooted at docs-site and deployed on Vercel; the /demo route is a backend-free snapshot using sanitized static data and browser-only interactions.
structured_candidates.summary: The docs site is a separate Next/Fumadocs app on Vercel; /demo is backend-free.

sources.excerpt: docs-site Next/Fumadocs on Vercel; /demo static snapshot

structured_candidates.canonical_text: Refactor: CLI command parsing was moved out of the monolithic entrypoint into support modules; runtime.rs now keeps the shared command types and dispatch helpers. Memories citing the old single-file layout should be updated.
structured_candidates.summary: CLI parsing moved into support modules; runtime.rs keeps shared types and dispatch.

sources.excerpt: command parsing split into support modules; runtime.rs = shared types + dispatch

structured_candidates.canonical_text: The release workflow verifies git tags, built packages, GitHub release assets, and the Homebrew formula before a release is considered ready, so a published version always has matching artifacts across channels.
structured_candidates.summary: The release workflow verifies tags, packages, GitHub assets, and the Homebrew formula.

sources.excerpt: verifies tags, packages, GitHub assets, Homebrew formula

structured_candidates.canonical_text: Incident: a 401 'invalid api token' surfaced when a repo-local skill talked to the installed prod service while the dev service was running on a different port and token. The fix was to make skill helpers resolve the dev CLI and its token first.
structured_candidates.summary: A 401 came from mixing dev/prod services; fixed by resolving the dev CLI first.

sources.excerpt: 401 from mixed dev/prod services; resolve dev CLI + token first

structured_candidates.canonical_text: Insight: Memory Layer's safety posture is consistent across surfaces — consolidation, curation replacements, loop automations, and MCP writes all produce human-gated proposals rather than silent mutations. The unifying rule is that durable state changes are reviewable, and the open tension is latency when a reviewer is not present.
structured_candidates.summary: Every durable state change in Memory Layer is a human-gated, reviewable proposal.

sources.excerpt: consolidated from consolidation, curation, automation, and MCP safety memories
